frameworks.name: efficient coding of subjective value
frameworks.one_line: subjective valuation as information-maximizing inference under limited coding capacity

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference of value
frameworks.one_line: valuation as inference accounting for environmental value structure

core_question: Can preference-based decision variability be explained by information-maximizing inference over subjective values under capacity limits?
key_assumptions: coding resources for value are limited
valuation reflects inference over the environmental distribution of values
decoding is statistically optimal given the encoding
manipulations: stimulus value structure tied to environmental statistics
conditions yielding a predicted preference illusion
key_findings: model captures variability in subjective value reports and preference choices
predicts a novel preference illusion confirmed empirically
same model accounts for confidence levels of value reports
interpretations: preference-based decisions reflect information-maximizing transmission and optimal decoding of value
apparent irrationalities follow from capacity-limited efficient value coding
out_of_scope_note: F1000/Faculty Opinions recommendation commentary on Polania et al.; synopsis reflects the recommended paper's abstract content.
